raw_user_request: Make a small browser game where a pilot rescues stranded pods.
concrete_goal: Build a local browser-playable rescue game where the player pilots a small ship, collects stranded pods, avoids debris, scores rescues, and can pause or restart from the first playable screen.
success_criteria: The first screen is a playable game state with visible objective, controls, score, lives, pause, and restart.
The player can complete the core loop by moving, collecting a pod, avoiding debris, scoring, and reaching a win or loss state.
Invalid or failure actions provide feedback, including debris collision, missed pod, and blocked movement.
Desktop keyboard input and mobile touch controls are covered when the game is run in the browser.
scope_in: Single-screen web-game runtime, HUD, local assets or placeholders, and playtest evidence.
Core rescue loop, scoring, lives, pause, restart, win, lose, and responsive input behavior.
scope_out: Online leaderboards, accounts, analytics, multiplayer, paid assets, external asset fetches, and deployment.
assumptions: The game uses local placeholder assets unless repo-owned assets already exist.
A local browser preview is enough for the first iteration.
Mobile input means a touch viewport check, not native app packaging.
implementation_constraints: Do not use network, credentials, paid AI generation, paid asset stores, or deployment.
Keep all game assets local and document asset provenance.
Do not accept build, lint, or test output without a playable local playtest.

target_users: Casual desktop and mobile browser players who want a short arcade rescue session.
product_brief.core_problem: The player needs an immediately understandable rescue challenge with clear controls, visible scoring, and quick restart.
primary_workflows: Start the game, pilot the ship, collect pods, avoid debris, and score rescues.
Pause, resume, restart, and recover from collision or loss states.
Use keyboard controls on desktop and touch controls on mobile viewport.
domain_assumptions: Arcade rescue vocabulary includes ship, pod, debris, lives, score, wave, pause, and restart.
The game should communicate objective and failure through HUD text and immediate feedback.
content_data_model_assumptions: Entities include player ship, rescue pod, debris hazard, score, lives, wave timer, and game state.
States include start, playing, paused, won, lost, restarted, asset loading, and recoverable render error.
non_goals: No multiplayer, online score sync, account profile, external asset pack, production deployment, or monetization.

ux_workflow_completeness: The rescue loop is playable from the first screen without a marketing landing page.
visual_polish: The first game screen shows a blue ship marker, yellow pod beacon, red debris hazard, fixed score badge, lives badge, and collision message so rescue, hazard, and status hierarchy are inspectable.
responsive_desktop_mobile: Desktop and mobile viewports keep the playfield, HUD, and controls usable.
accessibility_basics: Controls are labelled, keyboard operation is documented in the UI, focus is visible where DOM controls exist, and status text is readable.
error_loading_empty_states: Start, loading, paused, win, lose, restart, and recoverable missing-asset or render-error states are represented when applicable.
text_overlap_layout_stability: HUD labels, pause/restart controls, and mobile touch controls do not overlap the playfield incoherently.
domain_fit: Labels, goals, feedback, and assets fit an arcade rescue game.
evidence_requirements: Desktop screenshot or visual inspection, mobile screenshot or visual inspection, full playthrough notes, input coverage, performance/render stability, and asset integrity evidence.

target_player: Casual browser players using keyboard on desktop or touch controls on mobile.
game_brief.core_loop: Pilot the ship, collect a rescue pod, avoid debris, bank score, survive the wave, and repeat until all pods are rescued or lives run out.
mechanics: Directional movement, collision checks, collectible pods, moving debris, lives, score, and wave completion.
rules: Collecting a pod adds score and advances rescue progress.
Hitting debris removes a life and shows collision feedback.
Rescuing all pods wins; losing all lives ends the run.
controls: Desktop uses arrow or WASD movement plus pause and restart buttons.
Mobile uses on-screen directional controls plus pause and restart buttons.
win_lose_restart: Win after all pods are rescued, lose after all lives are gone, and restart resets score, lives, pod positions, and hazards.
difficulty_progression: Each rescue or wave increases debris speed or count within a readable arcade range.
scoring: Pods award points, survival bonus is shown at wave end, and collision penalty is visible through lives.
feedback: Collect, collision, score change, life loss, win, lose, pause, and restart each produce visible HUD or animation feedback.
assets: Use generated local raster sprites for the ship, pod, and debris when raster art is needed; route those assets through game_asset_worker with documented prompts, saved paths, and provenance.
engine_constraints: Browser runtime only, local preview, no external network, stable desktop and mobile rendering.

scenario_flows.name: Complete one rescue loop
preconditions: The local game preview is loaded at the first playable screen.
steps: Move the ship with desktop controls.
Avoid debris until the score and rescue progress update.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The pod is collected, score/progress feedback appears, the game remains playable, and pause/resume preserves state.
failure_or_edge_cases: Collision with debris removes a life and provides feedback.
Trying to move past a boundary is blocked without breaking controls.

scenario_flows.name: Reach resolution and restart on mobile
preconditions: The game is open in a mobile viewport with touch controls visible.
steps: Use touch controls to collect the remaining pods or intentionally collide until the run ends.
Observe the win or loss state.
Confirm score, lives, pods, and hazards reset.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The game reaches a clear win or loss state, restart returns to a fresh playable state, and touch controls remain usable.
failure_or_edge_cases: Missing local asset or render failure shows a recoverable error state.
HUD and touch controls do not cover critical playfield content.

verification_matrix.build: Run the project build command when present or document why no build command exists.
verification_matrix.lint: Run lint or equivalent static checks when present.
verification_matrix.test: Run automated tests when present, especially for scoring, collisions, state transitions, or input helpers.
verification_matrix.run: Start a local browser preview or open the static artifact and report the URL or path.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Play through core loop, invalid/failure action, scoring/progression, win/lose, pause/restart, desktop input, mobile input, performance/render stability, and asset integrity.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop 1440x900 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing the rescue ship, pod beacon, debris hazard, score badge, lives badge, pause button, and restart button.
Mobile 390x844 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing touch controls, rescue HUD, pod target, debris hazard, and readable win or loss message without overlap.
Manual workflow behavior check: start the rescue game, move the ship, trigger pod rescue, trigger debris collision, pause/resume, restart, and confirm score, lives, and rescue progress updated.
Accessibility check artifact covering keyboard controls, visible focus on pause/restart buttons, labels for mobile direction buttons, and contrast for score/lives badges in the rescue HUD.
State coverage check notes for start, playing, paused, won, lost, restarted, asset_loading, and render_error states in the rescue game.
Performance/render trace or inspection artifact confirming a nonblank stable rescue playfield and responsive controls.
Asset integrity audit artifact confirming local ship, pod, debris, and HUD assets or canvas shapes load with known provenance.

iteration_policy.threshold_rule: overall_completion_score must be at least quality_score_threshold.
iteration_policy.rework_rule: Any failed playtest, game-quality, asset, performance, scenario, or app-quality evidence creates concrete rework before acceptance.
stop_conditions: Review is accepted with all required app and game evidence present.
A network, external cost, deployment, or credential blocker is discovered.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Finalize game brief, core loop, controls, state list, and playtest scenarios.
Implement the playable rescue loop, HUD, controls, scoring, state lifecycle, and local run target.

Use $imagegen built-in mode to generate project-bound raster ship, pod, and debris assets, persist them in the workspace, document prompts, saved paths, transparency processing, provenance, integration notes, and load checks.
Playtest desktop and mobile core-loop, failure, win/lose, pause/restart, input, performance, and asset integrity.
Review game feel, HUD readability, responsive layout, and evidence gaps without overriding final review gates.
Apply review gates, including app-quality, game-quality, playtest, asset, and performance blocking gates.
